Review Title: Great product super clean easy install, for anyone
Review Description: This stand is very well built and engineered. I am seriously not kidding. I got things to do, but when a product is made and designed very well, I believe I have a choice. I chose to do the right thing and let others know about them.I’m a mechanical guy by trade. I won’t lie to anyone. I have to really stress this through. It was 0 of my own mechanical abilities that put this together. I have done many TV mounts for family ever since they came out. The first ones too, when all the ones people could afford it was a very lucky day if you received English ones and if you did, if they even made sense.I’ll be brief, I truly believe any teenager or adult that can turn a screw driver and and Alan key can put this together . Male or female, no matter. I did a brand new pioneer 43in on it today by myself and I’m retired and disabled now. If you’re TV is bigger than your will need one more person to hold the Television while making the final attachment to the top. 5 minutes TOPS. The rest is one person. I’m retired because of falling from 3 stories and half a century. I had 0 issues.Everything fits the way it should. All the hardware you could need for the sizes of tv specified are there. The instructions are super simple and have nice illustrations that make for ease of installation for anyone. If I buy another new TV this stand will be purchased with it. It’s clean has a shelf, Velcro straps to hide wires. This thing was made correctly. Point blank. $40 bucks and a sturdy clean looking install, way worth it for me.I don’t have any affiliation with this company and there not giving me a prize. I thought I would just let people know wassup, because I believe it’s the right thing to do PERIOD.Read more

This is a great compact stand for people who are short on space (living in apartments or dorms) and want to have their TV tucked away when they are not using it.I mounted a 55" TV on here and fortunately it is very stable. I have it set up on carpet and I am not worried about it falling down. I wouldn't say I feel the most comfortable with pushing the TV around for long distances because I am concerned that it is so top heavy, but I have rolled it room to room on occasion slowly and it has held up.Setting it up was fairly easy, most of it can be done by one person aside from actually mounting the TV. If you are looking to change the height of the TV often, this may not be the right option for you as it requires you to remove the TV and re-mount it onto the stand, which will take 5-10 minutes. There are essentially 2 height options that you can use here, so it's not the most versatile option of all, but I haven't found a need to adjust the height since mounting it for the first time.Overall it's great if you're looking for a stable space-saving solution to mounting your TV. It may not be the most aesthetic option, but it gets the job done.Read more

Review Title: Solid TV Stand — Perfect for Moving Your Screen Anywhere
Review Description: I set up the BONTEC Mobile TV Stand in my screened-in patio so I can watch games while lounging in my hot tub, and it works really well. Assembly was straightforward, and the stand feels sturdy and secure. My 32" TV fits perfectly, and the tilt function lets me angle the screen just right without any fuss.Rolling it around is easy, which is great if I want to shift it for shade or rearrange the patio. The adjustable shelf is handy to hold the contol away from the hot tub.Why Not an A-?The only reason I knocked it down a notch is the cable management. It’s a bit tight, so thicker HDMI or power cables can be a hassle to get organized neatly. Other than that, it does exactly what I need: sturdy, mobile, and perfect for casual patio TV sessions.Read more

Review Title: The perfect solution for my needs with 55" TV
Review Description: This is a great stand. I am using in a Sunroom with a  55" Hisense 4k TV and it fits the bill perfectly. Well constructed, all metal and substantial frame. The Hisense TV seems to be a little lighter than some of the major brands but it's for occasional use primarily for sports. Having the ability to move the TV around on the wheeled stand really matches my needs and I have no worries that it will tip over. Of course if it does, thats totally on me, not Bontec, they are meeting the needs of the consumer. I purchased this model because it has 3 adjustments, height, swivel left and right as well as a small degree of tilt. Works very well as advertised. I was mildly concerned that a minimum 51" height was too high but that is not actual height, it is from roughly the middle of the TV, mid-Visa mount. The bottom of the 55" screen is about 10-12 inches lower so using that, the height from the bottom to the floor is more like 40", a perfect height for viewing while sitting. I will probably never raise the TV on the stand to max height but could by about another 10" if needed. Full thumbs up to this Bontec stand.Read more

Review Title: Brand New iPhone 17 Pro Max With Visible Dead Pixel – Replacement Denied Despite Proof
Review Description: The media could not be loaded.I purchased a brand new iPhone 17 Pro Max from Amazon, and within days of delivery the device started showing a visible red/dead pixel issue with intermittent flickering. The defect is clearly visible to the naked eye and appears across multiple screens and colors.I immediately followed the correct process and visited an Apple Authorized Service Center. The issue was officially recorded in their service report and even captured on video. However, after keeping the phone for two days, the service center verbally denied the issue and refused to issue a defective certificate, which Amazon requires for replacement.Because of this, I am now running out of my replacement window, despite the product being defective from the beginning. I had to take leave from work, visit the service center multiple times, and still ended up helpless.This is a premium flagship device, and such a defect on a brand new phone is unacceptable. Even more disappointing is how difficult it has been to get a fair replacement after following all official steps.I expected much better quality checks and post-sale support for a product of this price.Buyers beware: Please check your device very carefully immediately after delivery and be prepared for a long struggle if you receive a defective unit.Read more

Review Title: Overall you can should buy realme 15x , or u can buy this also but i suggest realme 15x .
Review Description: First of all, I would like to talk about my Realme Narzo 80 Pro 5G. Overall, it is a very good phone in the 20–22k INR price range. I have been using this phone since 17 August 2025, and today is 28 November 2025—almost 4 months. The gaming performance is very good. The camera quality is also very good in bright environments such as sunlight or outdoors, especially when taking photos of people. Nature photography is also decent. Also sound quality is also good , i'm reading some negative comments , but i want to tell to whomever reading this review , The phone has dual stereo speaker support also , with 300% voice boost .I have the 8 GB RAM + 128 GB storage variant. I strongly suggest buying the 256 GB storage variant because in the 128 GB version, a large portion of the storage is taken by the system—around 30–40 GB. So, you only get about 60–70 GB of usable space, which is quite low for modern usage. Even though the phone is advertised as 128 GB, you don’t actually get the full 128 GB.In terms of performance, I did not face any heating issues. The phone stays cool during normal and even heavy tasks. It only heats slightly while charging, which is completely normal for fast-charging phones. The connectivity is also very good, with strong Wi-Fi and Bluetooth performance. Storage transfer speeds are fast as well, thanks to UFS 3.1 storage.I also appreciate the battery capacity, which is quite good for modern phones (6000 mAh). The battery lasts around 20–21 hours with moderate usage such as YouTube, Instagram Reels, or studying on the PW app. However, I have one issue with the screen. Although the display is very bright, easy to use in sunlight, and has excellent color quality, I would advise avoiding phones with curved displays. If a curved screen breaks after dropping on a cemented road or anywhere else, it is very difficult to find a duplicate replacement screen in the market.Additionally, you cannot expand the memory using an SD card, and there is no audio jack on this phone. On the positive side, the 80W charging is amazing—it charges the phone up to 25% in just 10 minutes and more than 60% in under 30 minutes.Overall, the phone is very good, but I still recommend checking out the Realme 15x. It offers almost the same features and is even better in some aspects because it doesn’t have a curved display. The Realme 15x also comes with 60W SUPERVOOC charging and a massive 7000 mAh battery, which is extremely impressive and lasts even longer.Read more

Review Title: Brand New iPhone 17 Pro Max With Visible Dead Pixel – Replacement Denied Despite Proof
Review Description: The media could not be loaded.I purchased a brand new iPhone 17 Pro Max from Amazon, and within days of delivery the device started showing a visible red/dead pixel issue with intermittent flickering. The defect is clearly visible to the naked eye and appears across multiple screens and colors.I immediately followed the correct process and visited an Apple Authorized Service Center. The issue was officially recorded in their service report and even captured on video. However, after keeping the phone for two days, the service center verbally denied the issue and refused to issue a defective certificate, which Amazon requires for replacement.Because of this, I am now running out of my replacement window, despite the product being defective from the beginning. I had to take leave from work, visit the service center multiple times, and still ended up helpless.This is a premium flagship device, and such a defect on a brand new phone is unacceptable. Even more disappointing is how difficult it has been to get a fair replacement after following all official steps.I expected much better quality checks and post-sale support for a product of this price.Buyers beware: Please check your device very carefully immediately after delivery and be prepared for a long struggle if you receive a defective unit.Read more

I ordered this cellphone about a month back and had a horrible experience to say the least. I had tremendous trust and confidence on Samsung products as they were extremely durable and of good quality. However, all this shattered with this cellphone purchase. Just after five days or so, the screen/display started malfunctioning. When I paid a visit to the service centre they simply updated the software and sent me back saying it will work. The very next day the cellphone's display again started giving me problems. I again went to the service centre and to my shock - they were not ready to acknowledge the hardware fault. After a lot of struggle with the service centre guys they changed the display. It's been a 5-6 days since and I can say with surety that the device is of low hardware quality. The speaker sound, display, infact everything feels like it's of low quality. I amazed at the kind of experience I have had with Samsung this time. I really wouldn't recommend anyone to buy this product. Guys the service centre guys were behaving as if they didn't care a damn. I have come down to a conclusion that it's not just the hardware but the service of Samsung has declined over the years to a point that they were behaving like a tin pot company. Extremely disappointed, sad but it's 100 % TRUE ! I would definitely stay away from buying Samsung products in future - a company on which I had tremendous trust and confidence ! I am already acknowledging this as money down the drain !! Even if Samsung would have exchanged the device for a new one, I still don't think it would have worked really well  ! But this is something that they should have done - to say the least !Read more

I am reviewing this phone after a few days of usage.This is a compromised or cut-down version of the normal 5G phones and hence it's available for less than 10K INR.The built-in hardware test is removed.  Dialling *#0*# does nothing.It has a very basic raw uncalibrated Gyroscope.Camera is okay. Despite 128GB ROM, 6GB RAM & 2 GHz processor the phone is surprisingly slow.If the signal quality is around 3 bars (may be because you are indoors or so) the signal falls back to 4G & mobile Internet practically does not work. See the speed test screenshot. The test fails to connect as there's no Internet even though mobile data is on at 4G connection. I tried every suggestion given by AI even including replacing the SIM to a new one but nothing helps.  This device has a weaker antenna or reception from technical point of view.Sadly the speaker sound is not that great. It's again poor. If you are in next room watching TV and phone rings in another room, you might miss a call because the speaker loudness is poor even at full volume.When you take a photo, the phone makes a Click sound. There's no direct setting to turn it off but there's a trick (available on Youtube videos ) that works and you can mute the Click sound.Battery life is average. Charging time is okay with a standard normal charger.Only one good thing about it is that as of now there's no beep or call recording announcement when you record a call. This might change in future though.Overall I am disappointed. Earlier I had Redmi 7A that did not have enough space and was overall slow in all aspects, so I bought this one but overall I am not at all impressed. I just feel I still have the same old experience with just added space, nothing more!!Read more

Review Title: Amazing battery and performance, okay camera.
Review Description: This is a great phone overall, the battery is the best I've ever had in a smartphone. I fully charge it in the morning and when I return home in the evening at 7 pm, the battery is still around 80%! I routinely end the day at around 65% battery, it can last 2 full dyas on normal usage. The charging is also super fast, it goes from 0 to 65 in under 30 minutes, and the fast charger is included in the box.The screen is fantastic, 120 Hz display and bright colours. The performance is also top notch, the phone feels very snappy and quick, I don't bother closing apps because the phone can hold as many apps as I want without slowing down. The only thing that's not the best in class, is the camera. The main camera is still very good, 50 megapizels and takes great photos, which are processed with AI to enhance their features. The ultrawide is not that good, and the front camera is average for the price. It's not a bad camera setup at all, still very good, but certainly below what you expect from a 50k phone.In summary, performace, battery and screen are amazing, camera is good but not great. If you want a reliable and fast phone that has a monster battery and super fast charging, go for this one.Read more

Review Title: Bought it for its battery back-up but got more
Review Description: Let me start with the elephant in the room claim - 24 hours battery back up from buds alone. I received this TWS around 6:45 in the evening, took about a couple  minutes to check the box contents and make sure the TWS was in perfect condition. Paired them with my phone only to find out the earbuds had 100% charge, so I started using them. I initiated my "testing" at 7 p.m. with The Jurassic Park (1993) at about 80% volume. 2 hours later, the buds had ~93% charge left. I said, "Challenge accepted!", and then watched the sequel, The Lost World (1997). Another 2 hours and some odd minutes later, the time was 11:10 p.m., the left bud showed 86% battery and the right was at 85%. At this point, I could see myself losing the battle, but I didn't wanna give up. I thought to myself, "Maybe if I tried harder, I'd win." But time, as always, wasn't on my side. It was 11:20 p.m., the silence of the night could be heard loudly and the fatigue of the day was catching up to me, when suddenly, an idea popped into my head. I rushed over to YouTube, searched for a 10 hour heavy metal music video, hit the play button, increased the earbuds volume to 100%, and then put both my phone and the buds on the bedside table grinning to myself thinking, "Yeah, survive that.", and called it a night. The morning came and as my vision cleared up to show 7:20 a.m. on the clock, I reached out to my phone, assuredly and confidently convinced, I had won the war.I didn't. The right bud was at 41% and the left at 45%. At this juncture, I gladly accepted my defeat and decided to write this story, a story of a brave TWS, a story that will never be in any history books, a story where the eternal TWS won against its sadistic owner.As for the rest, I will keep it brief;In-ear Detection: Works very well, no complaints.Sound Quality: Signature mode is too bass-y, not for me, for sure. Balance mode does the job and it does it well.Call quality: THE BEST. Tested against Boat Rockers 255 Neo, Boult Audio X50, Dizo GoPods Neo, Realme T100.Build Quality: Buds good. Case lid, when closed, has a slight wobble to it. Everything else is upto the mark.One final remark would be in-ear fitting, and thus, noise isolation. I find these buds to provide noise isolation as good as Dizo Gopods Neo with its 25Db ANC mode on. It almost feels like they were custom made for my ears. But do keep in mind that this can and will differ from person to person.Thank you for your time and I hope I didn't waste it.Read more

Review Title: The Ultimate Disappointment: A Scathing Review of the Worst Earbuds I’ve Ever Used
Review Description: The media could not be loaded.Let me preface this review by saying I’ve tested countless earbuds over the years, ranging from budget-friendly options to premium audiophile gear. Never in my life have I encountered a product as fundamentally flawed, poorly designed, and outright infuriating as these earbuds. If you’re considering purchasing these for gaming purposes, casual listening, or even as a backup pair—STOP. Save your money, your sanity, and your ears. From the abysmal mic functionality to the laughable sound quality, and the build quality that feels like it was engineered by someone who’s never held a pair of earbuds before, this product is a masterclass in failure. Let’s break down why these earbuds are an insult to the very concept of functionality, clarity, and performance.1. Mic Functionality: A Joke for Gaming PurposesLet’s start with the mic functionality, a feature that’s absolutely critical for gaming purposes or even casual Zoom calls. If you’re buying earbuds to communicate with teammates, stream, or participate in online meetings, prepare to be humiliated. The microphone picks up every conceivable noise except your voice. Background chatter, the faint hum of your refrigerator, even your dog barking three rooms away—all of it is crystal clear. But your actual voice? Muffled, distorted, and buried under a layer of static that makes you sound like you’re speaking through a tin can underwater.During a critical raid in my favorite MMO, my teammates repeatedly asked, “Are you chewing gravel? Why does your mic sound like a broken walkie-talkie?” When I switched to these earbuds’ mic functionality, the response was unanimous: “Never use these again.” For gaming purposes, where clear communication is non-negotiable, this mic is a liability. Even after tweaking settings, updating drivers, and screaming into the microphone like a madman, the clarity never improved. It’s almost impressive how a company could engineer a microphone this bad.2. Gaming Purpose? More Like “Game Over”Speaking of gaming purposes, these earbuds fail on every front. Latency is so severe that actions in-game—like gunfire or footsteps—are delayed by a full second, making competitive play impossible. Imagine hearing an enemy’s shotgun blast after you’ve already been respawned. The lack of clarity in spatial audio means you can’t distinguish between sounds coming from your left or right, a death sentence in shooters or horror games.But the real kicker? The performance of these earbuds under load is laughable. After just 30 minutes of use, they overheat to the point of discomfort, and the audio begins to crackle like a campfire. For a product marketed toward gaming purposes, the complete disregard for basic functionality is staggering.3. Functionality: A Textbook Example of “How Not to Design Earbuds”The functionality of these earbuds is so counterintuitive, it feels like a prank. The touch controls are hypersensitive yet unresponsive—a paradox that defies logic. Adjusting the volume? Prepare for a 50/50 chance of either muting the audio entirely or triggering your voice assistant. The companion app, which promises to “enhance your experience,” is a glitchy mess that crashes more often than a toddler’s toy car.Even basic functionality like pairing the earbuds to a device is a nightmare. They disconnect randomly, refuse to reconnect unless reset, and occasionally pair to the wrong device altogether (yes, my smart fridge now has audio privileges). For a product that claims to prioritize performance, the sheer lack of reliability is inexcusable.4. Clarity? More Like “Muddy Mess”If you value clarity in your audio experience, steer clear. The sound profile is so unbalanced it’s comical. Bass is overblown to the point of distortion, mids are nonexistent, and highs are shrill enough to shatter glass. Listening to music feels like being trapped in a nightclub with blown-out speakers—except even nightclubs have better sound quality.For podcasts or audiobooks, the lack of clarity turns every narrator into a garbled mess. Voices blend into the background noise, and subtle details are drowned out by the earbuds’ own hissing. Even at maximum volume, dialogue feels distant, as if the speaker is standing in another room. The sound quality isn’t just bad—it’s offensive.5. Performance: A Race to the BottomThe performance of these earbuds is a slap in the face to anyone who’s ever used a functional pair. The battery life, advertised as “up to 8 hours,” barely lasts two hours at 50% volume. And charging them? The case takes longer to power up than a medieval siege engine. Even the earbuds functionality during calls is a disaster. Every conversation becomes a game of “Guess What I Said,” with the other person constantly asking you to repeat yourself.Under stress—like multitasking between apps or running background downloads—the performance nosedives further. Audio stutters, the mic cuts out, and the earbuds emit a high-pitched whine that could double as a dog whistle. For a product that claims to prioritize performance, it’s clear no one tested these under real-world conditions.6. Earbuds Functionality: A Comedy of ErrorsThe earbuds functionality extends beyond poor sound and controls. The ergonomic design is a joke. They don’t fit securely in any ear shape, requiring constant adjustments mid-use. During a jog, one earbud flew out and nearly became roadkill. The “sweat-resistant” coating? A myth. After one gym session, the left earbud shorted out, emitting a smell akin to burnt plastic.Even simple tasks, like skipping a track or answering a call, require Herculean effort. The touch-sensitive areas are tiny and poorly mapped, leading to accidental hangs-ups or unintended playlist shuffles. For earbuds functionality that’s supposed to simplify your life, these add nothing but frustration.7. Sound Quality: An Assault on the SensesThe sound quality is where these earbuds truly cement their status as the worst on the market. The audio is flat, tinny, and devoid of depth—like listening to music through a cardboard tube. Genres that rely on nuance, like classical or jazz, are rendered unrecognizable. Rock and pop tracks lose their energy, with guitars sounding like kazoos and vocals like autotuned robots.Even for non-audiophiles, the sound quality is unbearable. Watching movies? Dialogue is drowned out by inconsistent volume levels, and action scenes lack punch. The lack of clarity and dynamic range makes every audio experience feel like a punishment.8. Build Quality: Cheap, Flimsy, and Downright DangerousThe build quality is a testament to corner-cutting. The plastic feels like it was salvaged from a dollar-store toy, with seams that creak under minimal pressure. The charging case’s lid wobbles like a loose tooth, and the earbuds themselves are so fragile, I’m afraid to breathe on them too hard.Within a week, the right earbud’s mesh grill fell off, exposing the speaker to dust and debris. The charging pins corroded after minimal use, and the case’s hinge now emits a death rattle every time it’s opened. For a product that costs more than a meal for two, the build quality is unforgivable.Conclusion: A Perfect Storm of FailureIn summary, these earbuds fail at every metric: mic functionality, gaming purposes, functionality, clarity, performance, earbuds functionality, sound quality, and build quality. They’re poorly designed, shoddily constructed, and utterly unfit for any use case. The sound quality is grating, the mic functionality is a joke, and the build quality is a safety hazard.If you value your time, money, and sanity, avoid these earbuds at all costs. For gaming purposes, communication, or even passive listening, there are countless better options—including earbuds half the price. These aren’t just bad; they’re an insult to the very idea of technology.Final Verdict: 0/10. The only thing these earbuds excel at is making you regret your purchase.Read more

I recently used the Boult X Mustang Dyno earbuds and overall, they are a good option in the budget segment.The design is one of the best things about these earbuds. Review Title: AAn Audiophile’s Take: Surprisingly good, but don't expect 7Hz Zero 2 performance
Review Description: The Reference Point (My Daily Driver):I want to be completely honest about my sound standards. My daily drivers are the 7Hz Salnotes Zero 2 wired IEMs. If you own those, you know exactly what "top-notch" clarity and "pure bass" feel like—where you can literally feel the sub-bass texture without any distortion.​The Comparison (Realme vs. IEMs):I bought the Realme Buds T200x to see how they stack up against a proper wired setup.​The Bass: The Realme T200x is good. For a wireless budget earbud, the bass is punchy and enjoyable. However, it is not on the same level as the 7Hz. It gets "close" to that signature sound, but it lacks that deep, "pure bass" feeling you get from the Zero 2s. It’s smooth, but not as immersive.​The Clarity: This is where the difference is most obvious. The 7Hz has crystal clear vocals. The Realme T200x vocal clarity is decent, but slightly recessed in comparison.​The Reality: A normal user won't notice these details and will love the sound. But if you are coming from IEMs, you will notice the difference.​Design & Comfort:As you can see in my photos, the matte finish and design are excellent. They definitely feel more premium than the price suggests, and the case is compact and pocketable.​Verdict:Don't buy these expecting them to replace your 7Hz Zero 2s—wired audio is still king. But if you want a wireless pair that gets surprisingly close to that sound signature without the wires, these are a solid backup.​Pros:​Premium matte design.​Good bass for a wireless TWS.​No distortion at normal volumes.​Cons:​Vocal clarity isn't as sharp as wired IEMs.​Bass is good, but lacks the "pure" texture of high-end gear.Read more

For anyone looking for reliable TWS earbuds under ₹1,500, these are hard to beat.### 1. Sound Quality & BassThe 12.4mm Dynamic Bass Drivers really deliver. The sound is punchy and deep without being muddy. If you're a bass lover, you'll enjoy these. You can also customize the sound profiles using the realme Link App, which is a great touch for budget earbuds.### 2. Call Quality (4 Mics & AI ENC)The 4-mic system works surprisingly well. I’ve used these for office calls and while walking in light traffic. The AI ENC (Environmental Noise Cancellation) does a solid job of filtering out background hums and wind noise. My voice stayed clear on the other end, which is rare at this price point.### 3. Battery Life & ChargingThis is the standout feature. The 48 hours of total playback is no joke. I’ve only had to charge the case once in five days of heavy use. Plus, the fast charging (10 mins for ~5 hours of playback) is a lifesaver when you're in a rush.### 4. Build & ConnectivityDesign: The case is compact and the "Volt Black" (or Storm Grey) finish looks premium.Connectivity: It features Bluetooth 5.4 and Google Fast Pair, so it connects to my phone almost instantly when I open the case.Dual Device Pairing: This is a hidden gem—being able to stay connected to my laptop and phone simultaneously is very convenient.### Pros:Incredible 48-hour battery life.Strong, deep bass.Clear calls with the quad-mic setup.Dual-device connection support.IPX4 water resistance (good for workouts).### Cons:The case is a bit of a fingerprint magnet (glossy finish).No Active Noise Cancellation (ANC), but the AI ENC for calls makes up for it.Verdict:If you want a "no-nonsense" pair of earbuds with great battery life and clear calling, go for it. Review Title: Excellent, Worth The Money But Conditions Apply
Review Description: Long detailed review but it hope it offers some perspectiveEveryone's ear is different. There is no "best" earbuds. Whats best for me may not be best you. So first and foremost, decide what type of music you listen to and whats your purpose of buying a TWSMy review is based on comparison with Galaxy Buds Plus (2020 Flagship Buds from Samsung) which J have been using till date. So my expectation was set very high from the begining. So how does this stand in front of a flagship at 1/3rd of the cost ?1. Fit and Finish: Its about the same as Galaxy buds plus in terms of looks, size and fit (I use the largest eartips). So if you are happy with buds plus, you won't get any complains from with Buds Core2. In terms of features, you lose the flagship features in Buds Core. That means no wireless charging, no in ear detection for music. No even a charging cable 🥲 But thats about it. Instead, you get ANC, better mics, better ambient mode, better battery.3. Coming  to the most important part, the sound quality: Here I was surprised. The sound quality is almost same as the flagship buds plus. But with buds core, you get much better bass. You will love it. It doesn't overpower other instruments, vocals but you can feel the punchiness. But if you are someone who prioritizes bass above everything else, this isn't for you.A small caveat: I have been using Samsung S series since 2020. Samsung has its own codec (SSC). So to make use of the full capability of any Samsung TWS. you need a Samsung device. It will work on other phones but may not give the best experience. But if you have a Samsung phone, no meed to look anywhere else, except if you need bass only.Also try to stay away from marketing terms like Hi-Res support, LHDC, etc, especially in budget TWS or headphones. They are just codec support, which means your TWS can play Hi-Res audio files. To get real Hi-Res audio experience, you need bigger physical drivers (40mm or more), powerful DSPs, much bigger battery. Thats why when you turn on the Hi-Res codecs, battery will drain like money from your wallet after 1st day of the month, even in the XMs, QCs, Momentums.Its impossible for TWS to have that type of capability. At best, due to hi-res codec support, the DSP may upscale normal songs by, maybe 5-10%, but that will come at a cost of battery and little to no listening difference unless you are a big audiophile. Think from a practical point of view, hi-res music are always behind a paywall. How many people who are buying budget TWS/headphones will be going to pay for apple music or spotify premium or amazon HD music ?That bring said, neither buds plus has any hi res, nor the buds core will give it. I also have a Sony WH CH720N which is a full fledged ANC headphone and it doesn't have any hi res either. Neither the Sony ULT 900 which costs above 10k come with hi res certification.My take: If you like the sound, go buy it. If it comes with hi res codec support, take it as a bonus feature but don't make it a make a break decision. Support for hi res means nothing if the driver tunning itself is bad.4. Calling: Calling is better on buds core than buds plus because it has 3 mics per bud i.e. 6 mics. So no complains there.5. ANC: This is the most misunderstood feature and most people fall for gimmicks. Don't fall terms like 50db, 55db, 45db ANC. These numbers mean nothing. ANC is an algorithm. So the only metric that matters is consistency and how smart it is. An inconsistent ANC may work indoors but fail in traffic. Or work well in traffic but fail in a coffee shop. Also ANC isn't there to give you a feeling of empty space (thats what earplugs are for), its there so that the other party can listen to your voice clearly. Thats when the smartness of ANC becomes clear. A badly implemented ANC would simply cut all the sound that fall below certain db. This doesn't matter during music listening but becomes a horrible experience in calls because it will cut your voice too. Hence the statement "whats your purpose of buying a TWS" in the begining.Long story short, if your usage is calls as well as music and your environment is mostky indoors and some traffic (not the traffics in which everyone keeps honking), buds core ANC is more than enough to cut down approx 80% or more. But lets says your friend is talking near you while you are on phone, you and the other party may hear it. Thats not bad ANC, thats a compromise the ANC algorithm is making to preserve your voice clarity. Now other brands ANC algorithm may decide to cut down the voice. So you and other party will feel quiter but your voice will sound muffled to the other party. Now the Sony XMs, Bose QCs work better in these scenarios but they also cost 15x more. Thats what the extra money is for.Read more

Review Title: Absolutely Blown Away by the boAt Airdopes 300 - My New Audio Holy Grail! (Gunmetal Black)
Review Description: Absolutely Blown Away by the boAt Airdopes 300 - My New Audio Holy Grail! (Gunmetal Black)Rating: ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ (5/5)Okay, I need to rave about these! After seeing the hype (and that 5K+ bought last month stat!), I grabbed the boAt Airdopes 300 in Gunmetal Black, and honestly? They've exceeded every expectation. If you're looking for premium features without the insane price tag, stop scrolling – these are it.Here's why I'm obsessed:1.  Cinematic Spatial Audio is REAL: This isn't just marketing fluff. Watching movies or binge-watching shows is a whole new experience. The sound genuinely feels like it's coming from around you – immersive, deep, and incredibly engaging. Music benefits massively too, feeling richer and more layered than any earbuds I've owned before.2.  Battery Life That Defies Logic: 50 HOURS?! This is the absolute game-changer. The earbuds themselves last ages (around 10 hours on a single charge for me), but the charging case just keeps going. I charged the case fully over a week ago, used the buds daily for hours (commutes, workouts, calls), and I still haven't seen a low battery warning. It's liberating not to constantly worry about charging. MASSIVE win.3.  Crystal Clear Calls with 4-Mic AI ENx: Taking calls on these is a dream. I've tested them on busy streets, windy balconies, and even in a slightly noisy cafe – the person on the other end consistently says I sound clear and focused. The AI noise cancellation for the mics really works wonders. No more "Can you repeat that?" frustrations.4.  Fast Charge is a Lifesaver: Forgot to charge? 10 minutes in the case genuinely gives you hours (around 2-2.5 in my experience) of playback. Super convenient when you're rushing out.5.  boAt App = Awesome Customization: The app is simple but powerful. I love tweaking the EQ presets (bass boost for workouts, podcast mode for clarity) and customizing the touch controls. Finding the earbuds via the app is also a handy feature.6.  Rock-Solid Connection & Low Latency: Bluetooth 5.3 is flawless. Pairing was instant, and the connection stays strong even when my phone is in another room. Gaming and watching videos is seamless – absolutely zero noticeable lag or audio-video sync issues.7.  Comfort & Build: The Gunmetal Black looks and feels super premium. They fit snugly and comfortably in my ears, even during longer sessions or workouts. The IPX4 rating gives me peace of mind against sweat and light rain.8.  Great Sound Signature: Bass is punchy and satisfying without being overwhelming, mids are clear for vocals, and highs are crisp. Perfectly tuned for everything from EDM to podcasts.Minor Nitpick (because nothing's perfect):The touch controls are sensitive (great!), but there's a slight learning curve to avoid accidental pauses/skips when adjusting them. You get used to it quickly though.Verdict:The boAt Airdopes 300 deliver phenomenal value. You get flagship-level features – incredible 50H battery life, truly immersive spatial audio, excellent call quality with AI ENx, fast charging, app support, low latency, and a sleek design – all at a very reasonable price point. The hype is real, and the 5K+ purchases last month make total sense.If you're looking for reliable, feature-packed, great-sounding TWS earbuds that won't die on you halfway through the day, look no further. The Airdopes 300 in Gunmetal Black are an easy 5-star recommendation! boAt has absolutely knocked it out of the park with these. Highly impressed!Read more

Review Title: Excellent, Worth The Money But Conditions Apply
Review Description: Long detailed review but it hope it offers some perspectiveEveryone's ear is different. There is no "best" earbuds. Whats best for me may not be best you. So first and foremost, decide what type of music you listen to and whats your purpose of buying a TWSMy review is based on comparison with Galaxy Buds Plus (2020 Flagship Buds from Samsung) which J have been using till date. So my expectation was set very high from the begining. So how does this stand in front of a flagship at 1/3rd of the cost ?1. Fit and Finish: Its about the same as Galaxy buds plus in terms of looks, size and fit (I use the largest eartips). So if you are happy with buds plus, you won't get any complains from with Buds Core2. In terms of features, you lose the flagship features in Buds Core. That means no wireless charging, no in ear detection for music. No even a charging cable 🥲 But thats about it. Instead, you get ANC, better mics, better ambient mode, better battery.3. Coming  to the most important part, the sound quality: Here I was surprised. The sound quality is almost same as the flagship buds plus. But with buds core, you get much better bass. You will love it. It doesn't overpower other instruments, vocals but you can feel the punchiness. But if you are someone who prioritizes bass above everything else, this isn't for you.A small caveat: I have been using Samsung S series since 2020. Samsung has its own codec (SSC). So to make use of the full capability of any Samsung TWS. you need a Samsung device. It will work on other phones but may not give the best experience. But if you have a Samsung phone, no meed to look anywhere else, except if you need bass only.Also try to stay away from marketing terms like Hi-Res support, LHDC, etc, especially in budget TWS or headphones. They are just codec support, which means your TWS can play Hi-Res audio files. To get real Hi-Res audio experience, you need bigger physical drivers (40mm or more), powerful DSPs, much bigger battery. Thats why when you turn on the Hi-Res codecs, battery will drain like money from your wallet after 1st day of the month, even in the XMs, QCs, Momentums.Its impossible for TWS to have that type of capability. At best, due to hi-res codec support, the DSP may upscale normal songs by, maybe 5-10%, but that will come at a cost of battery and little to no listening difference unless you are a big audiophile. Think from a practical point of view, hi-res music are always behind a paywall. How many people who are buying budget TWS/headphones will be going to pay for apple music or spotify premium or amazon HD music ?That bring said, neither buds plus has any hi res, nor the buds core will give it. I also have a Sony WH CH720N which is a full fledged ANC headphone and it doesn't have any hi res either. Neither the Sony ULT 900 which costs above 10k come with hi res certification.My take: If you like the sound, go buy it. If it comes with hi res codec support, take it as a bonus feature but don't make it a make a break decision. Support for hi res means nothing if the driver tunning itself is bad.4. Calling: Calling is better on buds core than buds plus because it has 3 mics per bud i.e. 6 mics. So no complains there.5. ANC: This is the most misunderstood feature and most people fall for gimmicks. Don't fall terms like 50db, 55db, 45db ANC. These numbers mean nothing. ANC is an algorithm. So the only metric that matters is consistency and how smart it is. An inconsistent ANC may work indoors but fail in traffic. Or work well in traffic but fail in a coffee shop. Also ANC isn't there to give you a feeling of empty space (thats what earplugs are for), its there so that the other party can listen to your voice clearly. Thats when the smartness of ANC becomes clear. A badly implemented ANC would simply cut all the sound that fall below certain db. This doesn't matter during music listening but becomes a horrible experience in calls because it will cut your voice too. Hence the statement "whats your purpose of buying a TWS" in the begining.Long story short, if your usage is calls as well as music and your environment is mostky indoors and some traffic (not the traffics in which everyone keeps honking), buds core ANC is more than enough to cut down approx 80% or more. But lets says your friend is talking near you while you are on phone, you and the other party may hear it. Thats not bad ANC, thats a compromise the ANC algorithm is making to preserve your voice clarity. Now other brands ANC algorithm may decide to cut down the voice. So you and other party will feel quiter but your voice will sound muffled to the other party. Now the Sony XMs, Bose QCs work better in these scenarios but they also cost 15x more. Thats what the extra money is for.Read more

Review Title: Amazing Sound Quality & Features – Worth It with Minor Flaws

Also, while the mic is decent for calls and general use, it’s not the best—some may experience occasional issues with clarity.Overall, this is a fantastic product with rich features and great performance, just be mindful of the charging sensitivity. Still very much worth it in my opinion!Read more

Review Title: Great premium earbuds, just not perfect across all setups.
Review Description: I recently picked up the Samsung Galaxy Buds3 Pro and after a couple of weeks of use here’s how they stack up.Pros: Top-tier sound for the price – the dual-driver + high-res codec combo gives surprising detail. The microphone/call quality is excellent even in noisy backgrounds as they fit my ears perfectly hence passive noise cancellation is good too. If you’re in the Samsung ecosystem you’ll appreciate the seamless device switching and app features and the case supports wireless charging too.Cons: Fit may not be ideal for smaller ears, and the ANC, while good, doesn’t completely block out all ambient noise compared to the very best (compared to Buds2 Pro). Some features only work fully with Samsung phones like (SSC), so if you’re using iPhone or a generic Android you won’t get full value. The stem controls (pinch/swipe) have a learning curve and sweaty workouts may make them finicky. Battery life is meh for prolonged use (4-4.5hrs with ANC and 5.5-6hrs w/o ANC)Summary: One less star just because of the battery backup. If you own a Samsung phone and want wireless earbuds that push sound, features and ecosystem integration, the Buds3 Pro are a standout pick. If you’re device-agnostic or mainly use iOS, you might find more balanced value elsewhere (Airpods) — but you won’t go wrong with these if you find them on sale.Read more

Review Title: Absolutely Blown Away by the boAt Airdopes 300 - My New Audio Holy Grail! (Gunmetal Black)

Music benefits massively too, feeling richer and more layered than any earbuds I've owned before.2.  Battery Life That Defies Logic: 50 HOURS?! This is the absolute game-changer. The earbuds themselves last ages (around 10 hours on a single charge for me), but the charging case just keeps going. I charged the case fully over a week ago, used the buds daily for hours (commutes, workouts, calls), and I still haven't seen a low battery warning. It's liberating not to constantly worry about charging. MASSIVE win.3.  Crystal Clear Calls with 4-Mic AI ENx: Taking calls on these is a dream. I've tested them on busy streets, windy balconies, and even in a slightly noisy cafe – the person on the other end consistently says I sound clear and focused. The AI noise cancellation for the mics really works wonders. No more "Can you repeat that?" frustrations.4.  Fast Charge is a Lifesaver: Forgot to charge? 10 minutes in the case genuinely gives you hours (around 2-2.5 in my experience) of playback. Super convenient when you're rushing out.5.  boAt App = Awesome Customization: The app is simple but powerful. I love tweaking the EQ presets (bass boost for workouts, podcast mode for clarity) and customizing the touch controls. Finding the earbuds via the app is also a handy feature.6.  Rock-Solid Connection & Low Latency: Bluetooth 5.3 is flawless. Pairing was instant, and the connection stays strong even when my phone is in another room. Gaming and watching videos is seamless – absolutely zero noticeable lag or audio-video sync issues.7.  Comfort & Build: The Gunmetal Black looks and feels super premium. They fit snugly and comfortably in my ears, even during longer sessions or workouts. The IPX4 rating gives me peace of mind against sweat and light rain.8.  Great Sound Signature: Bass is punchy and satisfying without being overwhelming, mids are clear for vocals, and highs are crisp. Perfectly tuned for everything from EDM to podcasts.Minor Nitpick (because nothing's perfect):The touch controls are sensitive (great!), but there's a slight learning curve to avoid accidental pauses/skips when adjusting them. You get used to it quickly though.Verdict:The boAt Airdopes 300 deliver phenomenal value. You get flagship-level features – incredible 50H battery life, truly immersive spatial audio, excellent call quality with AI ENx, fast charging, app support, low latency, and a sleek design – all at a very reasonable price point. The hype is real, and the 5K+ purchases last month make total sense.If you're looking for reliable, feature-packed, great-sounding TWS earbuds that won't die on you halfway through the day, look no further. I recently used the Boult X Mustang Dyno earbuds and overall, they are a good option in the budget segment.The design is one of the best things about these earbuds. It offers up to around 60 hours of total playback with the case, which is very useful for daily use like online classes and music.  Fast charging is also helpful when you are in a hurry.The sound quality is decent but not perfect. The bass is strong and loud, which is good for songs and casual listening, but the vocals and clarity could be better. For normal users, it is fine, but music lovers may not be fully satisfied.For gaming, it performs well because of its low latency mode, so there is less delay between sound and visuals.  Calling quality is average; it works fine in quiet places but struggles a bit in noisy environments.One drawback is that it does not have active noise cancellation, only environmental noise cancellation for calls. Also, some users have reported connectivity or quality issues, so durability can be a concern.✅ Pros:Stylish Mustang designVery long battery lifeGood for gaming (low latency)Affordable price❌ Cons:Average sound clarityNo active noise cancellationBuild quality could be better⭐ Final Verdict:Overall, Boult Mustang Dyno is a good budget earbud for daily use, gaming, and casual music. If you prioritize comfort and prefer half-in-ear buds like I do, the Samsung Galaxy Buds 3 are a game-changer. I've always found traditional in-ear buds uncomfortable and struggled to find the perfect balance between comfort and sound quality. I previously used the Buds Live for their comfortable fit, but the sound didn't meet my expectations. I also have Soundcore in-ear models, which are good, but I couldn't wear them for extended periods.When Samsung announced the Galaxy Buds 3, I was eager to try them out, especially as I'm already embedded in the Samsung ecosystem. I prebooked them without hesitation, and I'm thrilled to say they’ve exceeded my expectations.The Galaxy Buds 3 are the best half-in-ear earbuds on the market. Their premium case and futuristic design fit snugly and comfortably in the ear, offering the feel of in-ear buds without the discomfort. Call quality is impressive, and listening to music is a treat with robust bass and well-balanced highs and lows. The active noise cancellation (ANC) is surprisingly effective, providing a quiet listening experience even in noisy environments.Features like AI voice commands work seamlessly, custom equalizer and 360-degree audio enhance the overall listening experience. The pinch & swipe gestures are intuitive and responsive, making it easy to control playback and manage calls. The neck stretch reminder is useful addition. Wireless charging is fast and compatible with other wireless and MagSafe chargers I use. Although it offers 2 to 3 fewer options compared to the Pro model in the Wear app, I find it perfectly adequate.In summary, if you're searching for comfortable half-in-ear earbuds with excellent sound quality and advanced features, the Samsung Galaxy Buds 3 are worth every penny. Look no further—these are truly fantastic!Read more

Review Title: Good call quality but stopped working in a mere 15 days. They replaced promptly.
Review Description: I initially wanted to buy the Buds 3 Pro but mistakenly purchased these in the hurry of Prime Day. However, I found the open design quite appealing. The sound quality is very good, not excellent, with noticeable ANC. The lows are loud, but the highs could use some improvement.The call quality is a significant improvement compared to all previous earbuds I've used, including the Buds 3 Pro and Sony WF-1000XM4.In terms of appearance, they look great, and I really love the case.Unfortunately, one earbud stopped working while I was at the gym. It wouldn't charge or be recognized by the Galaxy Wear app. I bought them on EMI, so now I'm stuck paying for something I can't use since parts aren't readily available at the service center. This kind of malfunction never happened with my previous earbuds, whether they were Realme, Samsung Buds 3 Pro, or Sony XM4. Because of this experience, I will refrain from buying new models on their release day from now on. I'm considering buying the new Nothing earbuds next time.Overall Ratings:Sound Quality: 4/5Appearance: 5/5Build Quality: 3/5 (one earbud stopped working)Comfort: 4/5 (open design allowed for longer wear without feeling air pressure, fit was great for me, never had issues doing jumping jacks or running. Needed to screw in sometimes to get the ANC right and align the sound opening to ear canals)After Sales Service: 4/5 (service centers are widespread in cities, but parts for this new model are not available)Update: they fixed and gave me back in 5 days.Read more
